author: 0xunclebill
content: Hmm I was able to sync a Prysm node yesterday

author: 0xunclebill
content: I think I prolly got lucky and checkpoint synced during an epoch with no skipped boundary slot

author: 0xunclebill
content: btw regarding checkpoint sync complexity, if we dont pre-emptively fetch the payload envelope from the checkpointz server we need special handling in range sync for importing the envelope at the checkpoint slot. its easier for us to just have checkpointz server enable `GET eth/v1/beacon/payload_envelopes/{block_id}`

author: 0xunclebill
content: we get rate limited pretty quickly by prysm. issue is theres only really one or two nodes in the devnet that can serve us data... ill add some tighter self rate limiting rates for now

author: 0xunclebill
content:
would be nice to relax these rate limits for small devnets in the future i think

also we've added a new by range request (envelopes) so thats going to eat at rate limits quicker than before

author: 0xunclebill
content: we synced!

author: potuz
content: When you range sync anyway you're making different requests aren't you? You can always request slot, slot+N for Payloads and slot+1, slot+1+N for blocks and works everytime

author: 0xunclebill
content: we have this concept of “batches” in our range sync impl which is a grouping of ByRange requests for blocks columns and envelopes for a given epoch. The batch ID allows us to keep track of all the different requests and we couple all the responses for a given batch id and import everything together

author: 0xunclebill
content:
The coupling logic also prevents us from importing columns without a block. or envelopes without columns and block. etc 

anyways we can special case the first envelope but it becomes much simpler if we just fetch the envelope from the checkpoint server before letting range sync take over

author: nflaig
content: is this intended for the devnet only? I'm pretty sure this breaks checkpoint sync with lodestar on mainnet as we expect the state to be advanced to the epoch boundary, also per `store_target_checkpoint_state` that seems right.

author: nflaig
content: I also found previous discussion regarding this here https://github.com/ethereum/beacon-APIs/issues/423

author: potuz
content: I merged it in develop, happy to discuss this today in ACDT but in Gloas advancing it is strictly incorrect. If clients want to advance it then we need to return the post Payload state. I don't mind this (although this will be a rather subtle change in Prysm) but technically this will make the checkpoint server return something that can technically be reorged by honest nodes

author: potuz
content: I think we all understand the nuances, but setting it up in the spec is a little awkward, probably better to have a full writeup instead of dicussing here in the air. I'll write a full document on this issue, and have it ready in an hour or so
